title: NeighborTable evicts oldest entry without considering link quality
description: In rust/lichen-node/src/routing.rs:80-94, when the neighbor table is full, the oldest entry by last_seen_ms is evicted. This may evict a high-quality parent candidate in favor of a newly-seen poor-quality neighbor. In RPL, this could cause route flapping: good parent ages out, poor parent added, poor parent evicted, good parent re-added. Consider: evict by composite score (age + ETX), or don't evict if existing entry has better ETX than incoming.
